weeks.label: Week 1
weeks.title: Turn the dial slightly
weeks.goal: Move from 5,000 to about 6,000–6,500 steps a day, add a little brisk walking.
weeks.notes: Use the talk test throughout. Brisk means you can still talk, but you would not want to sing.

tasks.title: Hit 6,000+ steps
tasks.description: Reach at least 6,000 steps across the day.
tasks.detail: A gentle lift from your current baseline.

tasks.title: 20–25 minute walk
tasks.description: Do 5 minutes easy, 10 minutes brisk, then 5–10 minutes easy.

tasks.description: Reach at least 6,000 steps across the day.

tasks.detail: Try to do it outdoors if possible.

weeks.label: Week 2
weeks.title: Build consistency
weeks.goal: Work towards 6,500–7,000 steps a day, extend the brisk sections slightly.
weeks.notes: Still controlled. The aim is consistency, not heroics.

tasks.title: Hit 7,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for about 7,000 steps today.
tasks.detail: A little more volume, still manageable.

tasks.title: 25–30 minute walk
tasks.description: Do 5 minutes easy, 12–15 minutes brisk, then 5–10 minutes easy.

tasks.title: Hit 7,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for about 7,000 steps today.

tasks.detail: An ordinary day with one focused session.

tasks.detail: A good day for a longer route.

weeks.label: Week 3
weeks.title: Introduce intervals
weeks.goal: Lift daily steps to about 7,500–8,000 and use short faster intervals to prepare for possible jogging later.
weeks.notes: Faster means brisk, not running. You should still feel in control throughout.

tasks.title: Hit 7,500–8,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for the top end if it feels comfortable.
tasks.detail: This is your new baseline range.

tasks.title: 30 minute interval walk
tasks.description: Repeat 5–6 times: 1 minute faster pace, 2 minutes normal pace.
tasks.detail: Begin and end with a few easy minutes.

tasks.title: Hit 7,500–8,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for the top end if it feels comfortable.

tasks.title: Hit 7,500–8,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for the top end if it feels comfortable.

tasks.title: 30 minute interval walk
tasks.description: Repeat 5–6 times: 1 minute faster pace, 2 minutes normal pace.
tasks.detail: Brisk for the fast minutes, not breathless.

tasks.title: Hit 7,500–8,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for the top end if it feels comfortable.

tasks.title: 30 minute interval walk
tasks.description: Repeat 5–6 times: 1 minute faster pace, 2 minutes normal pace.
tasks.detail: Third interval session of the week.

tasks.title: Hit 7,500–8,000 steps
tasks.description: Aim for the top end if it feels comfortable.

weeks.label: Week 4
weeks.title: Confidence and optional progression
weeks.goal: Make 8,000 steps feel normal, continue interval walking, and optionally test tiny jogging intervals if everything feels easy and controlled.
weeks.notes: Jogging is optional. Staying with walking intervals for another week is a perfectly good outcome.

tasks.title: Hit 8,000 steps
tasks.description: Make 8,000 your comfortable new baseline.

tasks.title: 30 minute session
tasks.description: Either repeat Week 3 intervals or include 30–60 seconds of very light jogging within the faster parts.
tasks.detail: Jogging is optional and should feel easy.

tasks.title: Hit 8,000 steps
tasks.description: Make 8,000 your comfortable new baseline.

tasks.detail: No pressure to jog if walking still feels better.

tasks.detail: Treat any jogging as an experiment, not a commitment.
